character_profile.revision_note: Revised after the Willow/Shack and later truth conversation: Marcus is owed a truthful ending, but not necessarily full operational/intimate disclosure. Cass owns timing and scope.
character_profile.profile_status: Strong abridged/full hybrid. Enough continuity exists for four files, but unknowns should stay open.

identity.relationship_status: Cassiopeia Vance's established romantic person / boyfriend / partner, now in a relationship-ending/clarification thread
identity.duration_with_cass: About nine months as of early September

family.father: Mid-rank Ministry, Department of International Magical Cooperation
family.mother: Wizengamot junior bench / old-family household authority depending source register
family.politics: Cautious-grey, not grey-going-dark; not Vance-brothers shape

identity.drift_warning: Use Slytherin. Treat any Ravenclaw label as drift/typo unless explicitly re-established.

character_profile.core_concept: Marcus is the quiet Slytherin who lets the room forget he is there: formal, pure-blood-correct, not warm, not cruel, and important because he does not flinch from Cass. He is not nothing, not evil, not Lucian, and not the enemy. But he is also part of the old life Cass is leaving: safety, fineness, and the kind of proper stillness that would have let her stay small.
character_profile.role_in_story: Marcus is a relationship, truth, and safety-scope anchor around Cass. His function is not villain, obstacle, joke, or erased boyfriend. His function is the person who mattered before Caleb and Mei became the people Cass ran to when she cracked. The live question is no longer whether Cass should ask herself where Marcus fits. She has named the shape. The live question is what truth is owed, what truth is safe, and what truth would become cruelty or danger if spoken in full.
character_profile.public_reputation: Neutral, formal, distant, pure-blood-correct. Takes House/family seriously. Not actively unpleasant or pleasant. Capable wand-work. Hard to get a handle on by design.
character_profile.private_reality: Not fully known. Best current read: Marcus absorbs Cass without challenging her, which stabilized her but may also have helped her remain small. He is not Lucian and would not drag Cass into overt cruelty; but he might have let her drift into a proper pure-blood life by degrees. Cass choosing him still matters. Cass leaving him also matters.

willow_shack_read.item: He would not have cleansed or harmed Cass like Lucian's world.
He would likely have let her stay small, doing nothing beautifully.
When Cass cracked, she did not go to Marcus; she went to Caleb and Mei.
Cass recognized that you run to whoever you trust to pull you out.

truth_conversation_update.item: Marcus is owed a truthful end, not humiliation.
Full disclosure of Caleb/Mei/the trio may be unsafe and unnecessary.
Marcus does not need names/details if those details expose Caleb, Mei, or Cass to Slytherin/social danger.
A clean breakup / true-enough ending with no operational detail is morally plausible.
The remaining question is what truth is safe, kind, and necessary.

live_cass_marcus_state.current_tracker_phrase: Marcus talk owed — Cass-owned timing and Cass-owned scope.

sample_lines.item: Waddell. Marcus Bletchley. Sunday seven o'clock, then.
That is not a conversation for the Hall.

avoid.item: Do not make him melodramatically jealous unless played.
Do not make him stupid or oblivious.
Do not make him secretly evil because he is Slytherin.
Do not make him already consent to everything Cass/Caleb/Mei may become.
Do not erase him from Cass's life.
Do not force full confession as the only moral option.
Do not frame bounded truth as cowardice if the detail would create danger.

social.item: Lets the room forget he is in it.
Understands Slytherin table etiquette and old-family quietness.
May talk enough that detailed disclosure to him carries social danger.

cass.item: Absorbs Cass without making a thing of it.
Chosen by Cass, which is meaningful.
Part of Cass's inner circle/trust set.
Represents safety/fineness/old-life stillness more than rescue.

sport_combat.item: Played Slytherin reserve/inter-House Quidditch pickup against Lin several times.

cassiopeia_vance.status: Established romantic partner whose relationship with Cass is now in truthful-ending/clarification territory.
cassiopeia_vance.dynamic: Marcus is the one who did not flinch and who gave Cass a kind of safety. He is not nothing. But Cass has now admitted that when she truly cracked, she did not go to Marcus; she went to Caleb and Mei. Marcus may have let her stay small and proper. That is not villainy, but it is not rescue.
cassiopeia_vance.truth_boundary: Marcus is owed an honest end, but not necessarily names, details, or operational disclosure of Caleb/Mei/the trio. Cass owns the timing and scope. The moral center is truth-with-boundaries, not hide-everything or confess-everything.

caleb_waddell.status: Cass-adjacent cohort member and one of the people Cass ran to when she cracked.
caleb_waddell.dynamic: Caleb can read that Marcus was safety, not salvation. He should not make Marcus into Lucian or a villain. He also should not decide Cass's disclosure scope for her.

mei_chang.status: One of the people Cass trusted to pull her out; clear-eyed boundary voice.
mei_chang.dynamic: Mei's corrected line matters: this is not a thing Caleb/Mei did wrong by existing. The later truth conversation adds: Marcus gets an honest end, but not necessarily the whole dangerous map.

lin_wan.status: Knows Marcus through pickup Quidditch and social read.
lin_wan.dynamic: Lin says Marcus is polite, quiet, pure-blood-correct, and not obviously the encouraging-a-threesome type from outside the room.

hufflepuff_table.dynamic: Consensus: not worst Slytherin, neutral/formal, capable wand-work, not warm/not cruel; Cass choosing him earns benefit of doubt.

project_anchors.hufflepuff_table_read: Sat 3 Sept: Marcus is not worst Slytherin, pure-blood-correct, distant, formal, neutral, capable, and Cass choosing him is evidence.
project_anchors.threesome_joke_oddity: Sat 3 Sept: Lin says alleged Marcus approval is not stable consent; Cass may have defused the joke or Marcus has an unknown side.
project_anchors.formal_introduction: Sat 3 Sept: Marcus comes to Cass's side and introduces himself formally to Caleb.
project_anchors.mei_lin_good_influence_read: Thu 8 Sept: Marcus is cautious-grey, stable-not-growth, does not flinch, may not fully see Cass.
project_anchors.cass_inner_circle: Cass's trusted circle includes Caleb, Marcus, Lin, Mei, and Davina.
project_anchors.hexfield_inaugural: Sun 4 Sept: Marcus plays on Purple team with Cass, Joanna, Edward.
project_anchors.willow_shack_correction: Fri 16 under/after the Willow/Shack thread: Marcus is safety/fine/not Lucian, but Cass did not run to him when she cracked; he would have let her stay small.
project_anchors.truth_conversation_correction: Later truth conversation: Marcus is owed a truthful end, but truth has scopes. He does not necessarily need names/details/Caleb-Mei map. Cass owns timing and scope.

item.event: Lin processes the old Cass/threesome joke and says Marcus is not, from outside the room, the encouraging-a-threesome type.
Hufflepuff-table read: neutral/formal, capable wand-work, not warm/not cruel, Cass choosing him matters.
Marcus formally introduces himself to Caleb at the Slytherin table and confirms Sunday seven o'clock.
Hexfield inaugural: Marcus plays on Purple team.
Mei/Lin analyze Marcus as pure-blood old Slytherin line, cautious-grey, stable-not-growth, someone who does not flinch.
Cass's Caleb/Mei/office/overnight thread creates Marcus knowledge and boundary stakes.
Cass accepts the shape: Marcus is not Lucian and not nothing, but he is safety/fine/old-life stillness; when she cracked, she went to Caleb and Mei.
The moral shape becomes truth-with-boundaries: Marcus is owed an honest end, not necessarily names/details/full operational disclosure. Cass owns timing and scope.

drift_flags.house: Marcus is Slytherin; stray Ravenclaw label is drift unless explicitly re-canonized.
drift_flags.not_villain: Do not make him evil because he is Slytherin/pure-blood.
drift_flags.not_irrelevant: Do not erase him because Cass has a Caleb/Mei thread.
drift_flags.not_lucian: Do not make him Lucian-shaped. Cass explicitly corrects that.
drift_flags.not_full_confession_required: Do not make full disclosure the only moral outcome. Names/details may be unsafe and unnecessary.
drift_flags.not_hide_everything: Do not make bounded truth into pure avoidance. Marcus is owed an honest end.
drift_flags.cass_owns_scope: Cass owns timing and scope of the Marcus truth.
drift_flags.stable_not_growth: Best current read: stable/fine/safety, but not rescue/growth.
drift_flags.cass_choice: Cass choosing him matters; Cass leaving him matters too.

rag_priority.item: Use Marcus to preserve the nuanced relationship/truth boundary around Cass.
Keep him quiet, formal, observant, and pure-blood-correct.
He does not flinch; whether he sees Cass fully is unresolved.
Do not make him a villain or a joke.
Correct current state: Marcus is owed a truthful ending, but not necessarily full disclosure.
A clean breakup / true-enough ending without operational detail is live and morally plausible.
Preserve him as Slytherin 7th-year and Cass's established person.
